steps.title: Find the specific obstacles Text 1 identifies
steps.content: Text 1 claims dating is unreliable at Sterkfontein because of two specific problems: mixed soil layers prevent stratigraphic dating, and animal bones may be relocated by flooding. These are the obstacles blocking reliable age assignment.

steps.title: Apply Text 2's solution to these obstacles
steps.content:
They would argue that their techniques are better suited than other methods to the unique challenges posed by the Sterkfontein Caves. says the researchers would argue their technique is better suited than other methods to Sterkfontein's unique challenges—and that's exactly what Text 2 demonstrates.

Cosmogenic nuclide dating analyzes the breccia surrounding the fossils, explicitly avoiding both the problematic soil-layer and animal-bone approaches.

The other choices either introduce unsupported claims (the unsupported corruption claim), contradict the solution (the combination-method alternative suggests combining the old methods rather than replacing them), or misstate the advantage (the direct-application alternative claims it works because applied “directly” rather than because it avoids the flawed methods).

That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
